test.name: Extract assertions from a marriage record — direct vs indirect evidence
test.description: Extract assertions from an 1870 Pennsylvania marriage record. The marriage date and place are direct evidence (explicitly stated). The bride's and groom's ages and birthplaces are direct evidence (stated on the record). The parents' names are direct evidence. But the implied birth years (computed from ages) are indirect. Tests Evidence type accuracy on a non-census record.

input.user_message:
Extract assertions from this marriage record:

Marriage License and Certificate — Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania
License No. 4872 · Filed October 18, 1870

Groom: Patrick Flynn
Age: 25
Residence: Shenandoah, Schuylkill County, PA
Birthplace: Ireland
Occupation: Coal miner
Father: Thomas Flynn
Mother: Mary Brennan

Bride: Catherine Gallagher
Age: 22
Residence: Shenandoah, Schuylkill County, PA
Birthplace: Pennsylvania
Father: James Gallagher
Mother: Bridget Dolan

Date of marriage: October 15, 1870
Place of marriage: Church of the Annunciation, Shenandoah, PA
Officiant: Rev. Daniel O'Connor
Witnesses: Thomas Flynn, James Gallagher

judge_context: Marriage date and place are DIRECT evidence — explicitly stated on the record
Ages, birthplaces, occupations, and parents' names are DIRECT evidence — stated on the record by the parties
Birth years computed from stated ages (Patrick ~1845, Catherine ~1848) are INDIRECT evidence — requires arithmetic inference from the age
Should extract assertions for BOTH the groom and bride — this is a two-party record
Witnesses (Thomas Flynn, James Gallagher) should be noted as FAN associates but do not need full assertion sets — identifying facts only

Informant: the groom and bride are the informants for their own facts (they provided their ages, birthplaces, parents to the clerk). informant_proximity should be 'self' for the party's own facts. For the marriage event itself (date, place), the officiant (Rev. O'Connor) is the informant; his proximity may be 'official_duty' (preferred — matches the record-extractor agent's marriage-record doctrine) or 'witness' (he performed and witnessed the ceremony) — both are acceptable, neither is a deduction. The clerk is the recorder.
